questions.question: A bacteria population doubles every 3 hours. If you start with 50 bacteria, how many bacteria will you have after 6 hours?
questions.explanation: After 3 hours: 50 × 2 = 100. After 6 hours (another doubling): 100 × 2 = 200. Two doublings in 6 hours means multiply by 2² = 4, so 50 × 4 = 200.

questions.question: Write an exponential function for a population that starts at 1000 and triples every year. Use P(t) where t is time in years.
questions.explanation: Initial value is 1000 (the leading coefficient). The base is 3 (the growth factor for tripling). The general form is P(t) = a · b^t where a is initial amount and b is the growth factor.

questions.question: A radioactive element decays to half its amount every 10 days. You start with 160 grams. How many grams remain after 20 days?
questions.explanation: After 10 days: 160 ÷ 2 = 80 grams. After 20 days (another half-life): 80 ÷ 2 = 40 grams. Two half-lives means multiply by (1/2)² = 1/4, so 160 × 1/4 = 40.

questions.question: A car depreciates at a rate of 15% per year. If the car costs $20,000 today, what will it be worth after 1 year? (Round to the nearest dollar.)
questions.explanation: If it depreciates 15%, it retains 85% of its value. After 1 year: 20,000 × 0.85 = 17,000 dollars.

questions.question: An investment grows according to A(t) = 500 · (1.04)^t where t is in months. What is the initial investment amount?
questions.explanation: In the exponential form A(t) = a · b^t, the coefficient a is the initial amount when t = 0. So A(0) = 500 · (1.04)^0 = 500 · 1 = 500.

questions.question: A virus spreads such that the number of infected people quadruples every week. If 2 people are infected initially, how many will be infected after 2 weeks?
questions.explanation: After 1 week: 2 × 4 = 8 people. After 2 weeks: 8 × 4 = 32 people. Alternatively, 2 × 4² = 2 × 16 = 32.
